Motion By Plaintiff And Respondent Prince M. Edmonds, Jr. To Consolidate Civil And Probate Cases; Memorandum In Support
Off Calendar Dec-06-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF PRINCE EDMONDS' Motion To Consolidate Civil And Probate Cases, as follows:

Plaintiff Prince Edmonds' motion to consolidate is denied. The motion is untimely. The two cases have been pending since February of 2019 and there is no explanation why this motion was not filed earlier. Consolidation will likely cause a continuance of the current trial date in the civil action and the petition to compel accounting in the probate action. While there is some factual overlap between the actions, the court believes that they are sufficiently discrete that consolidation is not necessary to preserve judicial economy, avoid duplicative work, or to prevent inconsistent rulings.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Set For Trial On Feb-10-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
